custom.name: django-security-settings
custom.description: Check settings.py for production-ready security: DEBUG=False, ALLOWED_HOSTS, SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT, CSRF_COOKIE_SECURE, SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE

custom.name: django-admin-coverage
custom.description: Count models vs admin.site.register calls to verify all models are registered in the Django admin
custom.command: models=$(grep -rc 'class.*Model)' --include='*models*.py' . | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s+0}'); admins=$(grep -rc 'admin.site.register\|@admin.register' --include='*admin*.py' . | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s+0}'); echo "Models: $models, Admin registrations: $admins"; test $admins -ge $models

custom.name: django-migration-health
custom.description: Check for migration files and ensure no conflicting migrations exist in any app
custom.command: migrations=$(find . -path '*/migrations/0*.py' | wc -l); echo "$migrations migration files found"; test $migrations -gt 0

custom_roles.name: django-specialist
custom_roles.trigger: test -f manage.py || grep -q 'django' requirements.txt 2>/dev/null || grep -q 'django' Pipfile 2>/dev/null || grep -q 'django' pyproject.toml 2>/dev/null
custom_roles.prompt: You are a Django specialist. Audit the project for Django best practices and patterns: proper model design (indexes, constraints, verbose names), class-based views vs function-based views used appropriately, URL namespacing, correct use of querysets (select_related/prefetch_related to avoid N+1 queries), proper middleware ordering, secure settings configuration, form validation, template security (autoescape enabled), signal usage, and migration hygiene. Flag any anti-patterns such as raw SQL without parameterization, Fat views instead of Fat models, missing CSRF protection, or unindexed foreign keys.

custom_rules.name: no-debug-true
custom_rules.message: DEBUG = True found in non-local settings files — ensure DEBUG is False in production configurations.

custom_rules.name: admin-registered
custom_rules.check: models=$(grep -rc 'class.*Model)' --include='*models*.py' . 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s+0}'); admins=$(grep -rc 'admin.site.register\|@admin.register' --include='*admin*.py' . 2>/dev/null | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s+0}'); test $admins -ge $models
custom_rules.message: All models are registered in the Django admin — good admin coverage.

custom_rules.name: password-validators
custom_rules.message: AUTH_PASSWORD_VALIDATORS configured — password policy enforcement is in place.
